name: Alto Turia and Collado de las Vigas Loop
name_original: Río Túria y el Castillo de Moya  — circular por Santa Cruz de Moya
description: This difficult 41.8-mile road cycling route in Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel features the Turia River, Castillo de Moya, and 338
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Moya traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, such as the Río Túria, and significant elevation changes marked by numerous collados and puertos. The region features a mix of natural and historical points of interest, including reservoirs like the Embalse de Benagéber. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from winding roads alongside water bodies to challenging climbs through hilly areas. Best road cycling routes around Moya The most popular road cycling route is Alto Turia and Collado de las Vigas Loop, a 41.8 miles (67.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbing through the Alto Turia region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Río Túria by the Embalse de Benagéber and Santa Cruz de Moya — circular from Landete, a difficult 95.1 miles (153.1 km) path. This route explores the Río Túria valley and passes by the Embalse de Benagéber reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Collado Calderón, Collado de las Vigas, and Puerto del Cabezo Loop, an 83.7 miles (134.8 km) trail leading through multiple mountain passes, often completed in about 7 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Moya is defined by challenging climbs over collados, scenic river valleys, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Moya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Moya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Moya?
A: There are nearly 30 road cycling routes around Moya, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Moya?
A: Road cycling around Moya is characterized by significant elevation changes and challenging climbs over collados and puertos. While there are a few moderate options, the majority of routes, 16 out of 29, are classified as difficult, making the region particularly appealing to experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Moya?
A: While Moya is known for its challenging terrain, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. However, most routes feature substantial climbing and are better suited for moderate to experienced riders.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Moya?
A: For experienced riders, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092969537/alto-turia-and-collado-de-las-vigas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Turia and Collado de las Vigas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 41.8 miles (67.2 km) with substantial climbing, offer a significant challenge. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092984469/collado-calderon-collado-de-las-vigas-and-puerto-del-cabezo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Calderón, Collado de las Vigas, and Puerto del Cabezo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 83.7-mile (134.8 km) trail through multiple mountain passes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that explore the Río Túria valley or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region features routes that wind alongside water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102695799/rio-turia-by-the-embalse-de-benageber-and-santa-cruz-de-moya-circular-from-landete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Túria by the Embalse de Benagéber and Santa Cruz de Moya — circular from Landete&lt;/a&gt; explores the Río Túria valley and passes by the Embalse de Benagéber reservoir, offering scenic views.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect on road cycling routes around Moya?
A: The road cycling routes around Moya offer diverse scenic viewpoints, from expansive vistas over river valleys like the Río Túria to panoramic views from various collados and puertos. You can also enjoy picturesque views of reservoirs such as the Embalse de Benagéber.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Moya?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Moya are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092969537/alto-turia-and-collado-de-las-vigas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Turia and Collado de las Vigas Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardenete Castle – Monasterio de Tejeda loop from Landete&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes around Moya?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore historical and cultural points of interest. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5707485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Moya&lt;/a&gt; and various charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landete&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5175833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algarra&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer views of these historical sites and traditional villages.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Moya?
A: The region's climate generally supports road cycling for much of the year. However, given the significant elevation changes and potential for challenging conditions, spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures and stable weather for tackling the longer and more difficult routes.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through or near the Old Town of Moya?
A: Yes, there are routes that allow you to experience the historical charm of the area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Moya – Dirt road to Moya loop from Moya&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you through this historic area.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Moya?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Moya, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the region's varied terrain, which includes challenging climbs, scenic river valleys, and routes around picturesque reservoirs, offering a rewarding experience for those seeking an active adventure.
tips: Beautiful climb that is made up of esses and that rises from the town of Santa Cruz de Moya. Port of more than five kilometers in the Túria river valley.
This panoramic point offers incredible views over the Alto Turia canyon. It is an ideal place to contemplate the imposing force of nature over hundreds of years shaping the landscape.
The ruins of the ancient Villa de Moya are one of the main archaeological sites of the Middle Ages. They date back to the 13th century and were declared a historical-artistic monument in 1982.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route, covering approximately 67.2 kilometers, typically takes about 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete. This duration assumes a very good fitness level due to the significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road cycling experience, requiring a very good fitness level. While it features mostly well-paved surfaces, be prepared for some unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to push your bike. The route includes a substantial elevation gain of over 1,030 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and notable points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse and scenic views, traversing areas along the Turia River with natural landscapes, mountains, and Mediterranean vegetation. You'll pass through forests, canyons, and pine forests, particularly in the Alto Turia landscape. Key highlights include the historic Old Town of Moya with its 13th-century castle ruins, the Collado de las Vigas, and a Viewpoint Over the Alto Turia Canyon, offering stunning vistas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Santa Cruz de Moya?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Santa Cruz de Moya serves as a common starting point for this circular route. You should be able to find suitable parking within the village, especially near the main access points or public areas, before beginning your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally from April to October. This period avoids the harsher mountain weather conditions that can occur outside these months, ensuring a more pleasant and safer cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling in the Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the park. However, always adhere to local regulations, respect protected areas, and stay on designated paths to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with official trails such as [CIMA CU03] Collado de las Vigas, Ruta del Turia y el Ebrón, Ruta del Talayón, and Ruta del Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel, among others.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for road cycling, and while dogs are generally welcome in many natural areas, it's important to consider the challenging terrain and traffic on road sections. If you bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, on a leash, and capable of keeping pace or being transported safely, especially on the unpaved segments.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: Santa Cruz de Moya and other small villages along or near the route may offer limited options for cafes, bars, or small shops where you can resupply with water and snacks. It's advisable to carry sufficient provisions for the entire ride, especially given the route's length and difficulty.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring a well-maintained race bike, appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes and a pump), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also recommended due to varying conditions.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Río Túria y el Castillo de Moya — circular por Santa Cruz de Moya" is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, typically Santa Cruz de Moya, allowing you to return to your starting point without needing additional transport.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint Over the Alto Turia Canyon
